reason: Moses demonstrates clear responsibility for his people's welfare through multiple actions: (1) He intercedes with God on behalf of the people when God threatens to destroy them (Exodus 32:11-13), appealing to God to remember His covenant promises to the patriarchs and asking Him not to destroy Israel; (2) He explicitly offers to make atonement for the people's sin, saying 'Now I will go up to the LORD; perhaps I can make atonement for your sin' (Exodus 32:30); (3) He takes decisive action to restore order and address the transgression. These actions exemplify a leader prioritizing and taking responsibility for the welfare of his people, even when they have committed grave sins.
